title: Blog Editorial
subtitle: Running surface for the Living Doc Compositor blog — pipeline, ideas, coverage, and voice invariants. Dogfooding the substrate on the blog that describes it.
scope: The Living Doc Compositor blog — both Series 01 (The Living Docs Journal) and Series 02 (Trackers). Editorial direction, post pipeline, voice rules, and coverage over time.
sourceCoverage: Seeded from the seven posts published through April 2026, the voice audit from the editorial distance-review, and the initial idea lane.
objective: Keep the blog editorially coherent and moving — posts landing on cadence, voice consistent across the series, coverage of key editorial topics complete, angles developed before they go stale.
successCondition: A reader at any weekly cycle sees what's in flight, what ideas are maturing, which topics are covered and which are orphan, and which voice rules are being upheld. An agent drafting or reviewing a post can load this doc and get typed working context for the whole editorial operation.

objectiveFacets.name: Substrate explained
objectiveFacets.description: The typed ontology — convergence types, invariants, periods, registry — is explained where a reader needs to understand what the tool actually is.

objectiveFacets.name: Concrete case
objectiveFacets.description: A specific worked example (Textual #5380, AI-and-labor tracker, AI labs watcher) that makes an abstract claim inspectable.

objectiveFacets.name: New category
objectiveFacets.description: When a genuinely new use category appears (monitoring, competitor-watcher), it gets named and framed.

objectiveFacets.name: Claim defended
objectiveFacets.description: The blog takes and defends positions (agreement is what's left; the artifact and the index are halves of the same quadrant; the registry is a language).

objectiveFacets.name: Reader onboard
objectiveFacets.description: A reader new to the substrate can be oriented — not feature-toured, but brought into the reason-to-care through pain and mechanism.

objectiveFacets.name: AI-pairing lift
objectiveFacets.description: How someone pairing with AI in their profession is specifically helped by the substrate — not abstract, grounded in the session-state problem.

objectiveFacets.name: Series threaded
objectiveFacets.description: Posts within a series reference each other coherently so a reader picking up №N after №N−1 feels continuity, not restart.

periods.summary: Baseline. Seven posts published through April 2026 (five in Series 01, two in Series 02). Voice audit landed; twelve voice invariants declared. Idea lane seeded with five angles. Weekly cadence starts next week.

invariants.name: 'Load-bearing' sparingly
invariants.statement: The phrase 'load-bearing' appears at most once per post, and at most once per three consecutive posts across the series. Replace with verbs that do the work (what carries weight here, what the argument turns on).

invariants.name: Italics reserved for introduced terms
invariants.statement: Italic emphasis is reserved for terms being introduced and for quoted phrases. No italics on abstract nouns for emphasis ('the why', 'a running picture') more than twice per post.

invariants.name: No stage directions for the reader
invariants.statement: Phrases like 'stopping here for a second', 'worth stopping on', 'worth noting', 'here's the thing' are cut. If a beat matters, write it more forcefully; don't announce that it matters.

invariants.name: Vary openings across the series
invariants.statement: No two consecutive posts open with the same pattern (first-person scene, 'a few months ago I was trying to', 'you are trying to'). Rotate among: specific scene, counter-claim, quoted statement, hard observation, concrete question.

invariants.name: 'Not X. Y.' earned, not performative
invariants.statement: The 'Not X. Y.' construction appears at most twice per post and only when the contrast is doing real argumentative work. Cut if it's just rhythm.

invariants.name: Break the three-beat habit
invariants.statement: When a paragraph ends with three parallel items, check the third. If it's padding for rhythm, cut it. Two-item contrasts are allowed to be two.

invariants.name: No throat-clearing honesty
invariants.statement: Cut 'to be honest', 'let me be honest', 'I'll be direct', 'honestly'. If the next sentence is honest, the preamble is performative.

invariants.name: Diversify closers
invariants.statement: No post reuses a closer-pattern already used in the previous two posts of the same series. 'That's when X starts paying off', 'naming the split is the thing that lets X', 'that's the substrate working correctly' — each once across the series, not per post.

invariants.name: Cut 'actually' as intensifier
invariants.statement: 'What it actually does', 'what you actually need', 'what this actually is' — remove 'actually'. The sentence is almost always stronger without.

invariants.name: Em-dashes with restraint
invariants.statement: At most two em-dash-parentheticals per paragraph. If a sentence has three, pick the one that matters and move the others to separate sentences or cut.

invariants.name: Concrete-to-abstract ratio
invariants.statement: Every 2–3 abstract sentences (substrate, surface, shape, layer, projection) get at least one concrete anchor — a fact, a name, a file path, a number.

invariants.name: No mid-paragraph definitions
invariants.statement: Drop-in 'X is a Y' definitions scattered through the body read as pedagogical safety-nets. Define in the lede if needed; trust the reader thereafter.

invariants.name: Every cycle has a period note
invariants.statement: When a weekly cycle closes, the editorial-notes section must carry a card for that period summarising what shipped, what slipped, and what voice drift was observed.

invariants.name: Every draft has a target period
invariants.statement: Every card in the pipeline with status other than published must name the period it's targeted for; drafts without a target get flagged.

invariants.name: Idea cards must be fresh or decided
invariants.statement: Idea-lane cards older than six cycles must be either promoted to pipeline or retired with a decision note. Ideas don't sit indefinitely.

callouts.title: How to read this doc
items: Pipeline is the landing view — every post in flight or published, with status and synopsis.
Ideas is half-formed angles; promotion to pipeline requires a target period.
Coverage map (derived) shows editorial topics × which posts carry them; orphans surface as gaps.
Invariants carry the voice rules from the April 2026 audit; apply during draft review.
Editorial notes is one card per weekly cycle; shipped/slipped/drift captured there.

sections.title: Post pipeline
sections.rationale: One card per post — shipped, in flight, or planned. content-production is the existing type for parallel content streams; content-lifecycle status carries the state machine from not-started through published.

data.name: The governance layer: turning living docs into a testable substrate
data.synopsis: A fingerprint, a skill (/crystallize), and a canvas — and why they matter more than the individual features suggest.
dependencies: First substantial Series 01 post; sets up the governance-layer concept later posts build on.

notes.text: Published 2026-04-10. Series 01 · №05.
Carries substrate-explained and claim-defended facets. File: docs/blog-governance-layer.html.

notes.title: Voice pass · 2026-W16 №4
items: v1 (load-bearing): 2 → 1. Cut the lede use ('the load-bearing idea is not any one of those' → 'the idea that ties them together is not any one of those'); kept 'Here is the load-bearing claim' as the one allowed use (it labels the claim rhetorically).
v9 (no 'actually'): 1 → 0. 'a substrate you can actually measure' → 'a substrate you can measure'.
v2 (italics scoped): 5 → 2. Cut three single-word emphases (not, is, seeing). Kept 'opinionated structure' / 'testable substrate' — those are the two poles the post's whole frame rests on.
v5 at cap (2), v3/4/6/7/8/10/11/12 all pass. Cleanest pass so far — a sign the earlier Series 01 posts were written with less drift once the governance-layer narrative was already in hand.

data.name: Skipping the archaeology before real work starts
data.synopsis: Every time you engage with an open-source issue, you pay an archaeology tax. A living doc is how we made that time collect into something a fresh session can load.
dependencies: Tentpole use-case post; features Textual #5380 as the worked example.

notes.text: Published 2026-04-17. Series 01 · №04. Featured post on blog.html.
Carries concrete-case and claim-defended. File: docs/blog-oss-issue-deep-dive.html.

data.name: Agreement is what's left: living docs in a world of capable agents
data.synopsis: As AI capability expands, the bottleneck on complex work stops being capacity to execute. It becomes capacity to agree on what to execute — and that is a social problem, not a technical one.
dependencies: Thesis post for the future-facing framing; 'artifact and the index' builds on it.

notes.text: Published 2026-04-19. Series 01 · №03.
Carries substrate-explained and claim-defended. File: docs/blog-agreement-is-whats-left.html.

notes.title: Voice pass · 2026-W16
items: v1 (load-bearing): trimmed 5 uses → 0. Section H2 renamed 'The claim the essay rests on'. Pull-quote cite updated. Body uses rephrased ('carries the weight', 'holds the composition together').
v2 (italics scoped): 8 abstract-noun italics removed. Kept only italics on first-introduction terms (named, bounded subgraph; view vs. record of choices).
v9 (no 'actually'): H2 'What the system is actually betting on' → 'What the system is betting on'.
v5 (Not X. Y.), v12 (mid-paragraph definitions): flagged but not edited this cycle — lower-severity and changing them shifts rhythm.

data.name: The registry is a language
data.synopsis: A shared semantic language is what lets many minds — and increasingly, many agents — operate from the same picture.

notes.text: Published 2026-04-19. Series 01 · №02.
Carries substrate-explained and claim-defended. File: docs/blog-registry-is-a-language.html.

notes.title: Voice pass · 2026-W16 №2
items: v1 (load-bearing): 0 uses. Clean.

v2 (italics scoped): 8 emphasis italics on abstract nouns/words removed. Kept: a language (thesis), semantic contract (introduced term), Tackling Complexity (book title), shared language (pull quote).

v5 (Not X. Y.): 5 uses → 2. Rewrote three: 'An entry is a small semantic contract, not a schema'; 'Adding the five was an edit to one file — no deploy, no renderer change, no rewrite of any skill'; 'comes down to who can extend the language, not to architecture.'
v3, v4, v6, v7, v8, v9, v10, v11, v12: all pass. v11 is notably strong (post is well-anchored with Textual #5380, specific type names, book/ops/research examples).

data.name: The artifact and the index: composing living docs with a knowledge graph
data.synopsis: Portable living docs and queryable knowledge graphs look like competing architectures. They are two halves of the same quadrant — and the split tells you what each is for.
dependencies: Builds on agreement-is-whats-left; names the context-graph quadrant explicitly.

notes.text: Published 2026-04-19. Series 01 · №01.
Carries substrate-explained, claim-defended, new-category. File: docs/blog-artifact-and-index.html.

notes.title: Voice pass · 2026-W16 №3
items: v1 (load-bearing): 2 → 0. 'That's the load-bearing purpose' → 'That's the core purpose'; 'that cheapness is load-bearing for the scaling story' → 'that cheapness is what makes the scaling story work'.
v9 (no 'actually'): 6 → 0. All six cut, including the section heading 'What a living doc is actually for' → 'What a living doc is for' and the dek phrasing.

v2 (italics scoped): 36 → 12 (66% cut). Kept quoted questions (the why-wouldn't-you-just scene echoes), table-cell labels (present/past/why in the four-quadrant table), introduced-term emphasis (Portable/Typed/Bounded triad, the Proof ladder / Capability surface / ... list, code anchor / scene anchor pair, living as thesis word), and Emil's quoted customer/supplier example. Cut 15 single-word/phrase emphases: what, why (x3 non-table), discovered, start, queried, read, already containing, outside, my, pattern, authoring, navigation, artifact, convergence types are shared, across.

data.name: One page per topic you follow
data.synopsis: Staying current on a debate you care about is scattered work that never accumulates. Here's what a single page looks like when it holds your running picture of a topic.
dependencies: Opens Series 02 · Trackers. AI-and-labor tracker is the concrete example.

notes.text: Published 2026-04-19. Series 02 · №02 (shifted from №01 when six-ai-labs landed).
Carries concrete-case, new-category, reader-onboard, series-threaded. File: docs/blog-one-page-per-topic.html.

notes.title: Voice pass · 2026-W16 №5
items: v9 (no 'actually'): 7 → 0. The post title itself carried 'actually' ('One page per topic you actually follow' → 'One page per topic you follow'); rippled the change through the H1, breadcrumb, title tag, and external references on blog.html and in this editorial doc's pipeline card. Also cut from two H2s ('What this actually gives you', 'Fork it for what you actually watch') and two body uses.

v2 (italics scoped): 11 → 5. Cut six emphasis italics (current, phrase, running, scoped, your, phrase). Kept five term-introductions — evolution since last period (×2), stance label, prior period, and the quoted resolution-criterion example.
v1 (load-bearing): 0 uses. v5 Not-X-Y: within cap.

Observation: the title change is the first v9 edit that rippled outside the post file. Worth flagging — titles containing 'actually' function as intensified phrases and cascade through navigation surfaces. Catch them before publish.

data.name: Six AI labs, one page
data.synopsis: Keeping current on what each frontier lab is actually betting on is load-bearing work for a lot of people in 2026 — and the tools to do it well don't exist. Here's a single-page tracker.
dependencies: Second Series 02 post; demonstrates the competitor-watcher category built for the six frontier labs.

notes.text: Published 2026-04-19. Series 02 · №01.
Carries concrete-case, new-category, reader-onboard, ai-pairing-lift, series-threaded. File: docs/blog-six-ai-labs.html.

sections.title: Idea lane
sections.rationale: Half-formed angles before they commit to the pipeline. Each card is one idea with a provisional take; promotion to pipeline requires a target period and a synopsis.

data.name: Shipping the /editor-voice skill, and what writing-against-it taught me

notes.text: Angle: take the voice audit from April 2026, turn it into a Claude Code skill that runs on drafts against the invariants in this editorial doc. Post explains the skill, the first-pass edits it surfaced, and what rules turned out to be brittle vs. load-bearing.
Candidate for Series 01 (field-note about building editorial infrastructure) or Series 02 if we frame it for writers.

data.name: A living doc for the blog itself

notes.text: Angle: the existence of this doc. Self-referential without being twee — how an editorial surface with typed pipeline + invariants + coverage changed the weekly rhythm. Include screenshots of the doc operating on itself.
Series 01 fits best. Ship after two-three weekly cycles so there's actual movement to describe.

Angle: five concrete instances — book-chapter doc, market-data-substrate tracker, face-recognition pipeline, policy-fight watcher, an AI-safety research-frontier tracker. Show the same convergence types carrying work across engineering, research, operations, and writing. The substrate-agnostic claim becomes checkable.
Cross-audience post. Candidate for a Series 02 piece or as an explicit series bridge.

Angle: the semantic-stability principle, told through the competitor-stance-track vs. expert-stance-track decision. Why crisp + distinct beat fewer + parameterized — even when the structures look identical. Grounded in the actual registry growth from April 2026.
Series 01 post. Builds on registry-is-a-language.

Angle: for the AI-pairing professional audience specifically. Opens with the five-whys root cause — session statelessness is the real bottleneck. Goes through why existing tools fail (Notion, Linear, Slack, CLAUDE.md) and lands on living docs as the specific shape. Could function as the flagship reader-onboard post.
Series 02 piece; competes with six-ai-labs for top of the series once published.

sections.title: Coverage map
sections.rationale: Derived view over objectiveFacets + coverage edges at doc root. Shows editorial topics × which posts carry them, plus orphan facets (no carrier) and over-covered ones (many carriers).

sections.title: Editorial notes
sections.rationale: One card per weekly cycle. Captures what shipped, what slipped, what voice-drift was observed, and what to focus on next cycle.

notes.text: First editorial cycle. Baseline captures seven posts published through April 2026 (five Series 01, two Series 02). Voice audit completed from distance-review of the series; twelve voice invariants plus three operational invariants declared. Idea lane seeded with five angles. This editorial doc itself is the dogfood example — written after the audit, not before.

Voice drift observed in the audit: recurring use of 'load-bearing' across posts; italic emphasis overload on abstract nouns; self-referential stage directions ('stopping here for a second'); same opener pattern in three of five recent posts; 'Not X. Y.' construction used as rhythm tic; three-beat lists where the third is padding.

notes.title: Edit pass №1 · agreement-is-whats-left
items: Primary violations: v1 (load-bearing × 5) and v2 (italics on abstract nouns × 8). Both fully fixed.
Minor: v9 (actually × 1) fixed. v5 (Not X. Y. × 3) and v12 (mid-paragraph definitions × 3) flagged but deferred.
Observation: v1 and v2 are the two rules that most visibly lift the writing. They should be the first pass on every draft.

notes.title: Edit pass №2 · registry-is-a-language
items: v1 clean (0 uses). Good sign — load-bearing tic isn't universal; it was acute in the thesis-heavy post.
v2 violations: 8 emphasis italics on abstract nouns/words. Fixed. Kept only the thesis 'a language', introduced 'semantic contract', book title, and pull-quote emphasis.

v5 violations: 5 uses of 'Not X. Y.' constructions. Fixed 3, kept 2 at the cap. Rewrote 'not a schema. It is a small semantic contract' → 'is a small semantic contract, not a schema'; reshaped the triple-not-fragment pattern into a single list; flipped the architecture contrast.
Cross-post pattern confirmed: v2 (italics) is the #1 recurring tic. v5 (Not X. Y.) is #2. v1 (load-bearing) is high-severity when present but localized to specific posts.

notes.title: Edit pass №3 · artifact-and-index
items: Heaviest pass yet. v1 × 2 → 0; v9 × 6 → 0; v2 × 36 → 12 (66% cut).

v9 (actually) was the surprise — six uses across the post, including the H2 'What a living doc is actually for' and the dek. Every single one was stronger removed. This post demonstrates the rule earning its keep by volume.

v2: italic inventory fell into five clean categories — quoted questions (keep), table-cell labels (keep), introduced-term triads (keep), quoted modes (keep), single-word/phrase emphasis on abstract nouns (cut, 15 of them).

notes.title: Edit pass №4 · governance-layer
items: Cleanest Series 01 pass. v1 × 2 → 1 (kept the section-labeling use); v9 × 1 → 0; v2 × 5 → 2 (kept the opinionated-structure / testable-substrate contrast that the whole post rests on).

notes.title: Edit pass №5 · one-page-per-topic
items: Title itself carried 'actually' — 'One page per topic you actually follow' → 'One page per topic you follow'. First edit that rippled outside the post file (H1, breadcrumb, title tag, blog.html index card, this editorial doc's pipeline name).
v9 × 7 → 0. Two H2s and two body uses also cut.
v2 × 11 → 5. Cut six emphasis italics; kept five term-introductions (evolution since last period, stance label, prior period, quoted resolution criterion).
Rule observation: titles containing 'actually' are the highest-leverage v9 catches because they cascade through navigation. Catch before publish.

notes.title: Five-post pattern · Series 01 + Series 02 №02
items: v2 italics: 8, 8, 15, 3, 6 (drift across Series 01; Series 02 №02 mid-pack). Dominant recurring tic.
v9 actually: 1, 0, 6, 1, 7 (most volatile rule; spikes unpredictably; can land in titles).
v1 load-bearing: 5, 0, 2, 2, 0 (localized to thesis-heavy framings).
v5 Not X. Y.: 3, 5, —, 2, — (shows up on two posts, absent on three).

Governance-layer (earliest, Apr 10) remains cleanest; one-page-per-topic (recent, Apr 19) had the most v9 drift. The hypothesis 'drift grows across the series' now has contradicting evidence from within Series 02 itself — need six-ai-labs data to sharpen.

notes.title: Focus for 2026-W17
items: Last pass: blog-six-ai-labs. Completing it gives full-series coverage and will settle the drift-over-time hypothesis.
Promote /editor-voice skill idea to draft.
Manual pass timing: averaging ~7 min per post across the five done so far.
Watch for title-level 'actually' specifically — it's the catch that cascades furthest.
